rows.changesVisibilityPermanentlyOrConditiona: persistent until config restore/reload/restart path applies
rows.actualCursorUsabilityImpact: highest: removes cursor rendering feedback directly
rows.keyboardCanStillTriggerRecoveryPath: yes, but recovery must not require visible cursor or Hyprland keybinds
rows.scope: persistent-config-only-with-potential-runtime-effect-pending-reload-or-restart; runtime mutation not allowed in this proof sprint
rows.watchdogRestoreEnough: not enough for enablement; restore proof must be paired with explicit no-visible-cursor confirmation model
rows.selectionReason: Excluded first because it can remove cursor visibility immediately and globally; requires explicit later approval.
proofRequirements: UI/review projection with cursor visibility warning
no active runtime mutation during tests
restore command must be shown before applying
warning must explain cursor may disappear or become hard to use
confirmation path must not require clicking keep with a visible cursor
timeout restore must be automatic if user cannot interact
result log must record whether confirmation or automatic restore occurred
rows.hyprmodSafetyInterpretation: HyprMod metadata improves label/default/description/bounds evidence but provides no cursor visibility recovery proof.

hyprmodMetadataAdopted.label: Inactive timeout
hyprmodMetadataAdopted.description: Seconds before hiding cursor (0 to never hide)
hyprmodMetadataAdopted.helpText: Seconds before hiding cursor (0 to never hide)

rows.hideTrigger: idle timeout after configured seconds; very low values could hide quickly
rows.changesVisibilityPermanentlyOrConditiona: conditional while idle until input moves/restores cursor behavior
rows.actualCursorUsabilityImpact: medium-high: numeric values can make cursor disappear after delay; bad values may make interaction confusing
rows.keyboardCanStillTriggerRecoveryPath: yes if out-of-band CLI/token path is independent of cursor visibility
rows.scope: persistent-config-only-with-potential-runtime-effect-pending-reload-or-restart; runtime mutation not allowed in this proof sprint
rows.watchdogRestoreEnough: fixture watchdog enough for future proof only; live proof must ensure timeout cannot require mouse/cursor interaction
rows.selectionReason: Rejected for first subset because numeric timing can hide cursor quickly; needs bound-specific proof and warning design first.
proofRequirements: UI/review projection with cursor visibility warning
no active runtime mutation during tests
restore command must be shown before applying
warning must explain cursor may disappear or become hard to use
confirmation path must not require clicking keep with a visible cursor
timeout restore must be automatic if user cannot interact
result log must record whether confirmation or automatic restore occurred
rows.hyprmodSafetyInterpretation: HyprMod metadata improves label/default/description/bounds evidence but provides no cursor visibility recovery proof.

hyprmodMetadataAdopted.label: Hide on key press
hyprmodMetadataAdopted.description: Hides the cursor when you press any key until the mouse is moved.
hyprmodMetadataAdopted.helpText: Hides the cursor when you press any key until the mouse is moved.

rows.changesVisibilityPermanentlyOrConditiona: conditional until mouse movement restores visibility
rows.actualCursorUsabilityImpact: medium-high: typing a CLI confirmation could hide cursor, so confirmation must not need cursor feedback
rows.keyboardCanStillTriggerRecoveryPath: yes, but future proof must show token confirmation does not depend on seeing cursor after key input
rows.scope: persistent-config-only-with-potential-runtime-effect-pending-reload-or-restart; runtime mutation not allowed in this proof sprint
rows.watchdogRestoreEnough: fixture watchdog enough for future proof only; live proof must model key-triggered cursor disappearance during confirmation
rows.selectionReason: Rejected for first visibility subset because it can hide cursor during keyboard confirmation; needs dedicated CLI-token usability proof.

no active runtime mutation during tests
restore command must be shown before applying
warning must explain cursor may disappear or become hard to use
confirmation path must not require clicking keep with a visible cursor
timeout restore must be automatic if user cannot interact
result log must record whether confirmation or automatic restore occurred
rows.hyprmodSafetyInterpretation: HyprMod metadata improves label/default/description/bounds evidence but provides no cursor visibility recovery proof.

hyprmodMetadataAdopted.label: Hide on touch
hyprmodMetadataAdopted.description: Hides the cursor when the last input was a touch input until a mouse input is done.
hyprmodMetadataAdopted.helpText: Hides the cursor when the last input was a touch input until a mouse input is done.

rows.actualCursorUsabilityImpact: medium: affects cursor visibility after touch, not immediately on apply
rows.keyboardCanStillTriggerRecoveryPath: yes; CLI/token recovery is keyboard/file based and does not require touch or visible cursor

rows.selectionReason: Selected as a future proof candidate because it is conditional on touch input and does not directly remove cursor visibility at apply time.
